memory-save.template: Use memory_add with fields from $ARGUMENTS. Expected format: title:<text> category:<text> tags:<comma,separated> content:<text>. If essentials are missing, ask only for missing values.

memory-list.template: Use memory_search and memory_list together. Rules: if args are empty, call memory_list(limit=20). If args include id:<number>, call memory_search with that id and mode=all. If args include '--mode all|summary', pass mode. If args include category:<name>, pass category. Remaining text is query. Default limit=8 for search.

memory-setting.template: Use memory_get_preference when args are empty. If args contain summary|all|off set default via memory_set_preference. Return current/default mode clearly.

memory-compress.description: Compress text or file content for storage
memory-compress.template: Use memory_compress with the text or file path provided. Returns a compressed base64 string suitable for memory storage.

memory-extract.template: Use memory_extract with the compressed base64 string to restore original content.

memory-compress-save.description: Compress and save text as a memory entry in one step
memory-compress-save.template: Use memory_compress_save with title and content. Optional: category, tags, level.

task-save.description: Save current task progress to memory (auto-save)
task-save.template: Use memory_add to save task progress. Category: session-state. Tags: auto-save, task-progress. Content: Brief summary of completed work and remaining tasks (max 500 chars).
